title: U Mass - Aspartate Transferase
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"f905d","text":"Summary: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dpahn","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"77u57","text":"This experiment involves a spectrophotometric measurement using Roche Cobas Clinical Chemistry Analyzer. Serum levels of aspartate transferase reflect liver function.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start:
{"blocks":[{"key":"1bbu8","text":"Notes:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":6}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"33j4h","text":"
√  Try to use freshly prepared serum and plasma samples for this assay.
√  No dilution or treatment of the sample is required, but plasma samples should be centrifuged to remove any fibrin/fibrinogen clumps.
√  Samples should be stored at 2-8°C for 24 hours prior to analysis. For longer periods, store samples at -70°C, and avoid repeated freeze/thaw cycles.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9kf5v","text":"√ A 50 µl dead volume is required in addition to sample volume for multi-protein analysis (typically 1-5 µl).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Perform daily quality control assessment of instrumentation before analysis.
Load each sample into a specialized micro-sample cup for the clinical chemistry analyzer.
Select Aspartate Transferase test on display and run the analysis.
creator: Lili Liang
